description_long: This is a freestanding, pyramid-shaped shelving unit with five progressively wider shelves from top to bottom, supported by two dark, angled side frames. The shelves are made of a rich, dark brown wood, while the side supports appear to be constructed from a sturdy, black metal. The open design allows for easy access to each shelf from the front, making it suitable for displaying or storing items in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or community spaces. The top shelf is the smallest, while the bottom shelf is the largest, creating a stepped, tiered effect. There are no doors, handles, or enclosed compartments, emphasizing its open and accessible nature. The overall structure is robust and stable, designed to support a variety of objects placed on its shelves.
description: A pyramid-shaped, open shelving unit with five wooden shelves and black metal side supports, designed for accessible storage or display in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width and tiered arrangement of the five shelves, with the black metal side frames forming a triangular outline.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the shelves and the vertical alignment of the metal supports, emphasizing the open, ladder-like profile.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the stepped shelves and triangular frame, with no back panel or enclosure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the depth and open structure of the shelving unit.
